title: How it works
subtitle: Walk through the path from a few sentences about your business to a working ERP.

steps.step1Body: Write a few paragraphs in your own language: what you do, which modules (inventory, invoice, HR) you want, and any special rules. No technical background required.
steps.step2Body: An input router agent decides which modules to enable, which entities are needed, and what's missing. If something is genuinely ambiguous, you get a few short clarifying questions.
steps.step3Body: Specialist agents for inventory, invoice, and HR produce fields, labels, relationships, and business rules in an SDF (System Definition File).
steps.step4Body: A deterministic integrator merges every module's output, resolves cross-references, and makes sure the assembler receives a consistent SDF ready for code generation.
steps.step5Title: 5. Preview and download in one click
steps.step5Body: Preview the generated ERP in your browser and, when you're happy, download it as a ready-to-run container bundle.

items.a: Inventory (multi-location, reservations, inbound/outbound, serial/expiry tracking), invoicing (customers, billing, payments, tax) and HR (employees, departments, leave, attendance).
English and Turkish for now. The language you pick when creating a project stays fixed for that project.

items.q: Where does the generated ERP run?
items.a: The output is a Docker Compose bundle you can download and run on your own server or locally.

cta.heading: Try it out
cta.body: Build your own ERP from a few sentences.
